key_features: Distinct, relatively smooth-edged central body drag mark
Wide track width, consistent with large sea turtle species
field_notes: The tracks indicate a large sea turtle came ashore and returned to the ocean. The pattern of the flipper marks is distinctly simultaneous, meaning both front flippers moved together, creating parallel impressions. A clear, somewhat smooth and wide central drag mark from the turtle's body (plastron) is visible between the flipper tracks. This combination is characteristic of a green sea turtle. There is some seaweed (sargassum) deposited along the high tide line, but the tracks are clear and undisturbed in the immediate area. The tracks appear fresh given their clarity and definition in the sand.
recommendation: Monitor the area for signs of nesting activity, such as a disturbed area where a nest may have been dug. If a nest is identified, ensure it is marked and protected from disturbance. Report the track sighting and any potential nesting activity to local wildlife authorities or a sea turtle conservation organization to contribute to monitoring efforts and ensure appropriate protection measures are taken, especially during nesting season.
